ucs.title: [LLM] Mofang spearphishing from victim-imitating look-alike sender domain
ucs.description: Hunts inbound spearphish where the sender's registered domain is a typo/lookalike of the recipient's own corporate domain - Mofang's signature tradecraft (the group is literally named for its habit of cloning a victim's infrastructure to deliver ShimRat/ShimRatReporter to Myanmar gov, defense, automotive and weapons targets).
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(All_Email.subject) as subject values(All_Email.file_name) as file_name values(All_Email.url) as url from datamodel=Email where (All_Email.file_name IN ("*.exe","*.scr","*.lnk","*.js","*.vbs","*.hta","*.docm","*.xlsm","*.rtf","*.iso","*.img","*.zip","*.rar","*.7z") OR All_Email.url="*") by All_Email.src_user All_Email.recipient | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Email)` | rex field=src_user "@(?<sender_domain>[^>\s]+)" | rex field=recipient "@(?<recipient_domain>[^>\s]+)" | eval sender_root=mvindex(split(sender_domain,"."),0), recipient_root=mvindex(split(recipient_domain,"."),0) | where len(sender_root)>=4 AND len(recipient_root)>=4 AND sender_domain!=recipient_domain AND sender_root!=recipient_root | eval near_match=if(like(sender_root,"%".substr(recipient_root,1,len(recipient_root)-1)."%") OR like(recipient_root,"%".substr(sender_root,1,len(sender_root)-1)."%") OR (abs(len(sender_root)-len(recipient_root))<=2 AND substr(sender_root,1,3)=substr(recipient_root,1,3)),1,0) | where near_match=1 | join type=left sender_domain [| tstats summariesonly=true min(_time) as domain_first_seen from datamodel=Web where Web.dest_domain="*" by Web.dest_domain | rename Web.dest_domain as sender_domain] | eval domain_age_days=round((now()-domain_first_seen)/86400,0) | where isnull(domain_age_days) OR domain_age_days<60 | table firstTime sender_domain recipient_domain sender_root recipient_root domain_age_days subject file_name url
ucs.kql:
let lookback = 14d;
let recipient_domains = EmailEvents | where Timestamp > ago(lookback) | summarize by RecipientDomain = tolower(tostring(split(RecipientEmailAddress,"@")[1])) | where isnotempty(RecipientDomain);
EmailEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where DeliveryAction !in ("Blocked","Junked")
| where AttachmentCount > 0 or UrlCount > 0
| extend SenderDomain = tolower(SenderFromDomain), RecipientDomain = tolower(tostring(split(RecipientEmailAddress,"@")[1]))
| extend SenderRoot = tostring(split(SenderDomain,".")[0]), RecipientRoot = tostring(split(RecipientDomain,".")[0])
| where SenderDomain != RecipientDomain and SenderRoot != RecipientRoot
| where strlen(SenderRoot) >= 4 and strlen(RecipientRoot) >= 4
| extend Prefix3Match = (substring(SenderRoot,0,3) == substring(RecipientRoot,0,3))
| extend ContainsRoot = (SenderRoot contains substring(RecipientRoot,0,strlen(RecipientRoot)-1)) or (RecipientRoot contains substring(SenderRoot,0,strlen(SenderRoot)-1))
| extend LenDiff = abs(strlen(SenderRoot) - strlen(RecipientRoot))
| where ContainsRoot or (Prefix3Match and LenDiff <= 2)
| join kind=inner (EmailAttachmentInfo | where Timestamp > ago(lookback) | where FileType in~ ("exe","scr","lnk","js","vbs","hta","docm","xlsm","rtf","iso","img","zip","rar","7z")) on NetworkMessageId
| project Timestamp, SenderFromAddress, SenderDomain, RecipientEmailAddress, RecipientDomain, Subject, FileName, FileType, SHA256, NetworkMessageId
| join kind=leftouter (UrlClickEvents | where Timestamp > ago(lookback) | project NetworkMessageId, ClickedUrl=Url, ClickAction=ActionType) on NetworkMessageId
ucs.rationale: Mofang is operationally distinguished from other Chinese clusters by infrastructure imitation - they pre-stage domains that look like the target's own (e.g. <victim>-mail[.]com, <vic-tim>[.]net) before sending the ShimRat lure. Pure 'malicious attachment from external sender' rules will not flag this because the sender appears to be the victim themselves; the lookalike-root + recently-registered +

ucs.title: [LLM] Mofang ShimRat persistence via sdbinst.exe loading user-writable .sdb
ucs.description: Detects installation of an Application Compatibility Shim Database (.sdb) from a non-standard path - the persistence mechanism baked into Mofang's bespoke ShimRat implant. Pairs the sdbinst execution with a recent .sdb write in user/temp space to surface the install chain.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_cmd values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name="sdbinst.exe" by Processes.dest Processes.process_guid | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rex field=cmdline "(?i)(?<sdb_path>[A-Za-z]:\\\\[^\"\s]+\.sdb)" | where isnotnull(sdb_path) | eval suspicious_path=if(match(sdb_path,"(?i)\\\\(AppData|Temp|ProgramData|Users\\\\Public|Roaming|Downloads|Desktop)\\\\") AND NOT match(sdb_path,"(?i)C:\\\\Windows\\\\AppPatch\\\\"),1,0) | where suspicious_path=1 | join type=left dest [| tstats summariesonly=true values(Filesystem.file_path) as sdb_written_path values(Filesystem.process_name) as sdb_writer earliest(_time) as sdb_write_time from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_name="*.sdb" by Filesystem.dest | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)`] | table firstTime dest user parent parent_cmd cmdline sdb_path sdb_writer sdb_written_path sdb_write_time
ucs.kql:
let win = 7d;
let sdb_writes = DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(win)
| where FileName endswith ".sdb"
| where FolderPath !startswith @"C:\Windows\AppPatch"
| project WriteTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, SdbFullPath = strcat(FolderPath, "\\", FileName), Writer=InitiatingProcessFileName, WriterCmd=InitiatingProcessCommandLine, WriterSHA=InitiatingProcessSHA256;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(win)
| where FileName =~ "sdbinst.exe"
| extend SdbArg = extract(@"(?i)([A-Za-z]:\\[^\"\s]+\.sdb)", 1, ProcessCommandLine)
| where isnotempty(SdbArg)
| where SdbArg !startswith @"C:\Windows\AppPatch"
| where SdbArg has_any (@"\AppData\", @"\Temp\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\Roaming\", @"\Downloads\", @"\Desktop\")
| join kind=leftouter (sdb_writes) on $left.DeviceId == $right.DeviceId, $left.SdbArg == $right.SdbFullPath
| extend AncestryLooksOfficey = InitiatingProcessParentFileName has_any ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessParentFileName, AncestryLooksOfficey, SdbArg, Writer, WriterCmd, WriterSHA, WriteTime
| union (
    DeviceRegistryEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(win)
    | where RegistryKey has @"\AppCompatFlags\Custom\" or RegistryKey has @"\AppCompatFlags\InstalledSDB\"
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("sdbinst.exe","msiexec.exe","trustedinstaller.exe")
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, RegistryKey, RegistryValueName, RegistryValueData, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine
)
ucs.rationale: Application Shimming for persistence is rare in the wild and was a defining capability of ShimRat - the implant Mofang has used since 2012 against Myanmar government/MoD and downstream defense/auto/weapons targets. Legitimate sdb installs originate from MSI/installers under C:\Windows\AppPatch with TrustedInstaller/MSIEXEC ancestry; sdbinst.exe loading an .sdb out of AppData/Temp/ProgramData (espe
